name: Sputofy
description: Sputofy Media Player is a self-hosted music player designed for seamless streaming across all your devices. Share your favorite tracks with friends and keep your library organized with customizable playlists. The intuitive home page makes discovering what to play next effortless, offering quick access to your most-loved songs and recommendations based on your listening habits. Enjoy a smooth, personalized music experience while staying in control of your collection.

name: Schmolax Metadata Editor
description: Schmolax Metadata Editor is a powerful tool for editing song metadata, allowing you to easily update and customize tags for MP3 and FLAC files. With built-in web scraping, it can automatically retrieve missing details like album art, artist names, and release dates. Its intuitive interface ensures quick and precise metadata management, helping you keep your music library well-organized and accurate with minimal effort.

name: Crawling Typer
description:
NPM crawling-typer is a js node package made to easily implement the stylish effect of typing an array of words one character at the time.
Quickly customize settings such as typing speed, delete speed, pause time between one word and the next, number of times the effect should loop and effect behavior after said number (if finite) of loops has concluded.

name: Automaton Designer
description:
A tool for designing and simulating finite state automata, made with C# and using Unity as a front end tool.
Features include:
Graphical design of automata
Simulation of automata

name: PoliTalks Ep. 40
description:
I was invited to participate in the 40th episode of PoliTalks, a podcast made by the students of Polimi for scientific dissemination. We talked about the evolution of 3D rendering and the history of gaming, and all the challenges and advancements that videogames brought through-out the years.
It was a great opportunity to share my insights and engage in a lively discussion with the hosts.
Check out the full unedited episode here -> Uncut version

link.preview: Listen to the episode on Spotify

name: Hardware Task Scheduler
description:
An RTL implementation of a highly optimized Hardware Task Scheduler (Priority Queue) written in VHDL. Designed to interface with an external Single-Port Synchronous RAM, the module efficiently manages up to 63 concurrent tasks with dynamic priority sorting and extraction.

To ensure zero-defect logic and mitigate edge-case race conditions (such as Delta Cycles), the project features a robust hybrid verification framework. A custom Python Golden Model generates thousands of randomized test vectors, which are dynamically validated by a VHDL Testbench using the std.textio library.

name: Galaxy Trucker
description:
A faithful digital adaptation of the popular board game Galaxy Trucker, developed in Java.
The project features a comprehensive implementation of the game's mechanics, including ship construction, resource management, and space exploration. Players can build their own spaceships, navigate through hazardous space routes, and compete against others in a dynamic and engaging environment. The game offers a 4 player multiplayer mode, allowing for a rich gaming experience that captures the essence of the original board game.

name: Movhex
description:
A high-performance algorithmic engine for optimal route calculation across a dynamic hexagonal grid. Developed for a simulated logistics network, the system models geographic maps as complex graphs to evaluate the most cost-effective travel paths.

The project showcases advanced Graph Theory applications, supporting dynamic topology updates such as modifying transit weights and toggling custom air routes between non-adjacent nodes. It efficiently processes shortest-path queries using custom Data Structures and Algorithms.

name: Project Management and Reporting
description:
A full-stack web application designed for custom hardware configuration. The platform allows authenticated users to build, manage, and clone personalized PC setups by selecting specific components (CPU, GPU, RAM SKUs) while dynamically calculating the total price.

The project was developed using a dual-architecture approach to showcase progressive web enhancement: a traditional Server-Side Rendered (SSR) version using pure HTML/Java, and a modern Single Page Application (SPA). The SPA leverages vanilla JavaScript and AJAX to perform asynchronous server requests, ensuring seamless and partial UI updates without full page reloads.

name: EmoProsopon
description:
State-of-the-art facial recognition system leveraging deep learning techniques to analyze and interpret human emotions.

The project utilizes convolutional neural networks (CNNs) trained on extensive datasets to accurately classify a wide range of emotional expressions, including happiness, sadness, anger, surprise, and more. With real-time processing capabilities, the system can be applied in various domains such as mental health monitoring, customer experience analysis, and human-computer interaction to provide valuable insights into emotional states.
